assignment05

Author

Xiaohan Xie 

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(GenomicRanges)
  library(chromVAR)
  library(SummarizedExperiment)
  library(edgeR)
  library(epiwraps)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(pheatmap)
})
Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.2

1. Download (a subset of) ATAC-seq peak counts in the hippocampus upon stress

(already in SummarizedExperiment format)

options(timeout=3600)
download.file("https://ethz-ins.org/content/mouse_mm38_hippocampus.peakCounts.SE.rds", "mouse_mm38_hippocampus.peakCounts.SE.rds", mode="wb")
se <- readRDS("mouse_mm38_hippocampus.peakCounts.SE.rds")
head(se)
class: RangedSummarizedExperiment 
dim: 6 12 
metadata(0):
assays(1): counts
rownames: NULL
rowData names(0):
colnames(12): Control_female_GSM6041841.bam
  Control_female_GSM6041842.bam ... FSS_male_GSM6041857.bam
  FSS_male_GSM6041858.bam
colData names(1): depth
colnames(se)
 [1] "Control_female_GSM6041841.bam" "Control_female_GSM6041842.bam"
 [3] "Control_female_GSM6041843.bam" "Control_male_GSM6041844.bam"  
 [5] "Control_male_GSM6041845.bam"   "Control_male_GSM6041846.bam"  
 [7] "FSS_female_GSM6041853.bam"     "FSS_female_GSM6041854.bam"    
 [9] "FSS_female_GSM6041855.bam"     "FSS_male_GSM6041856.bam"      
[11] "FSS_male_GSM6041857.bam"       "FSS_male_GSM6041858.bam"      
# add the metadata needed
row.names(se) <- as.character(granges(se))
se$group <- c("CTRL","CTRL","CTRL","CTRL","CTRL","CTRL","FSS","FSS","FSS","FSS","FSS","FSS")
se$sex <- c("female","female","female","male","male","male","female","female","female","male","male","male")

2. Using this object, run 2 differential analyses

2.1 Comparing stressed (denoted ‘FSS’ – forced swim stress) and control animals

dds_grp <- DGEList(assay(se), group=se$group)
dds_grp <- calcNormFactors(dds_grp)
dds_grp <- estimateDisp(dds_grp)
Using classic mode.
et_grp <- as.data.frame(topTags(exactTest(dds_grp), Inf))
head(et_grp,10)
                          logFC   logCPM       PValue        FDR
1:190561376-190561625  2.956480 5.568769 1.961625e-06 0.04052717
1:176712487-176712736 -5.547319 7.661587 3.602032e-05 0.13176183
X:134830585-134830834 -5.303583 6.889311 4.969718e-05 0.13176183
1:125542384-125542633 -4.828259 8.216940 5.289166e-05 0.13176183
1:38248121-38248370   -5.984659 8.519979 5.517843e-05 0.13176183
1:72830161-72830410    2.858128 4.136854 5.948103e-05 0.13176183
1:21635475-21635724   -4.385234 6.854042 6.447313e-05 0.13176183
1:159451546-159451795 -4.818483 5.728855 7.544813e-05 0.13176183
1:54734997-54735246   -4.283884 7.508608 7.707593e-05 0.13176183
X:16998632-16998881   -4.973348 7.122513 9.351076e-05 0.13176183
ggplot(et_grp, aes(logFC, -log10(FDR))) + geom_point()

Plotting the heatmap:

anno_col <- data.frame(
  Group = se$group
)
top_peaks_grp <- rownames(head(et_grp, 10))
# extract the logCPM 
log_counts_grp <- cpm(dds_grp, log=TRUE)
mat_grp <- log_counts_grp[top_peaks_grp, ]
rownames(anno_col) <- colnames(mat_grp)
pheatmap(mat_grp,
         annotation_col = anno_col,
         show_rownames = TRUE,
         show_colnames = FALSE,
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         main = "Top 10 Most Significant Regions (Control vs FSS) ")

2.2 Comparing male and female animals

dds_sex <- DGEList(assay(se), group=se$sex)
dds_sex <- calcNormFactors(dds_sex)
dds_sex <- estimateDisp(dds_sex)
Using classic mode.
et_sex <- as.data.frame(topTags(exactTest(dds_sex), Inf))
head(et_sex,10)
                        logFC   logCPM       PValue          FDR
Y:1010447-1010696    6.876619 6.682547 2.920870e-22 6.034518e-18
Y:1010170-1010419    6.864394 6.813826 3.240449e-19 3.347384e-15
Y:809076-809325      6.419731 5.522297 7.380945e-19 5.083011e-15
Y:1245354-1245603    6.184489 5.260409 4.044915e-17 2.089198e-13
Y:897407-897656      8.759947 4.548791 6.643047e-17 2.744907e-13
Y:1245678-1245927    7.175640 6.896536 9.138570e-17 2.984281e-13
X:50591034-50591283 -5.905083 5.983284 1.011131e-16 2.984281e-13
X:50583794-50584043 -5.473241 6.233290 1.249589e-16 3.031322e-13
Y:1286466-1286715    7.629468 6.982274 1.320518e-16 3.031322e-13
X:50590734-50590983 -5.172471 6.575802 1.783856e-16 3.355202e-13
ggplot(et_sex, aes(logFC, -log10(FDR))) + geom_point()

Plotting the heatmap:

anno_col <- data.frame(
  Sex = se$sex
)
top_peaks_sex <- rownames(head(et_sex, 10))
# extract the logCPM 
log_counts_sex <- cpm(dds_sex, log=TRUE)
mat_sex <- log_counts_sex[top_peaks_sex, ]
rownames(anno_col) <- colnames(mat_sex)
correct_order <- order(anno_col$Sex)
mat_sex_sorted <- mat_sex[ , correct_order]
anno_col_sorted <- anno_col[correct_order, , drop = FALSE]
pheatmap(mat_sex_sorted,
         annotation_col = anno_col,
         show_rownames = TRUE,
         show_colnames = FALSE,
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         main = "Top 10 Most Significant Regions (Female vs Male) ")
